core_mechanism: frequency-domain multipath interference of time-delayed contributions on a network, where linear scattering at graph vertices superposes signals carrying path-dependent phase delays, so that a reflection null occurs only when the delays synchronize and commensurable delay differences generate a periodic comb of frequency-selective cancellation zeros
brief_reason: The abstract reduces transparency at a junction to synchronization of path travel times, with the upstream reflection governed in frequency space by the width-weighted distribution of path delays and commensurable path-length differences producing a periodic comb of frequency-selective zeros.
